skill.name: writing-utils
skill.description: Guides the creation of small, modular utility functions for a Next.js application.
skill.summary: Create concise, modular utility functions for Next.js projects.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3, but the line only describes writing utility functions for a Next.js app. No cryptographic code, prompt injection attempt, network access, filesystem access, or external command behavior was found in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The reported location contains descriptive frontmatter text about writing utility functions. No weak cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation is present, so this is a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain metadata text and contains no code or algorithm reference. The full skill file has only general coding guidance.

content.value_statement: Utility code can become hard to maintain when related helpers grow in one file. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create focused Next.js utility functions.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of modular utility functions for a Next.js app.
Encourages splitting utilities across multiple files when useful.
Promotes concise files with a maximum target of 50 lines.
Supports cleaner organization for reusable frontend helper logic.
Provides lightweight coding rules that can be applied during implementation.
limitations: Does not provide ready-made utility function implementations.
Does not define a folder structure beyond separating utilities when possible.
Does not include testing guidance or validation requirements.
Does not specify TypeScript, JavaScript, or framework version details.

use_cases.title: Organize App Helpers
use_cases.description: Create small helper files for formatting, parsing, or shared Next.js logic.

use_cases.title: Refactor Large Utility Files
use_cases.description: Split a crowded helper file into focused modules with clear names.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Utilities
use_cases.description: Apply a simple rule set when adding shared helpers to a Next.js project.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Utility
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a small utility function for my Next.js app. Keep it modular and explain where the file should live.

prompt_templates.title: Split Related Helpers
prompt_templates.prompt: Write utility functions for these related tasks. Separate them into multiple files if that keeps each file focused and under 50 lines.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Utility Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for improving existing helpers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this utility code for a Next.js app. Refactor it into modular helper files while preserving behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Utility Module Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a feature area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a small utility module set for this Next.js feature. Propose file names, responsibilities, and concise helper functions.

output_examples.input: Create date formatting helpers for a Next.js dashboard.
output_examples.output: A focused date utility module with concise formatting helpers and a clear file placement recommendation.

output_examples.input: Refactor a large helpers file into smaller modules.
output: Short file descriptions for each new helper file.
Notes about preserved behavior and import updates.

output_examples.input: Plan utilities for form validation and display formatting.
output_examples.output: A small utility plan that separates validation helpers from display formatting helpers.

best_practices: Keep each utility file focused on one responsibility.
Use clear file names that describe the helper purpose.
Split helpers when a file becomes hard to scan or exceeds the size target.
anti_patterns: Do not place unrelated helpers in one shared dump file.
Do not create tiny files when one cohesive module is clearer.
Do not hide feature-specific business logic inside generic utilities.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps create concise, modular utility functions for a Next.js application.

faq.question: Does it generate complete app features?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on reusable helper functions and utility file organization.

faq.question: Can I use it with TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask for TypeScript types when your Next.js project uses TypeScript.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains only written guidance and does not request network access.

faq.question: How strict is the 50 line rule?
faq.answer: It is a target for concise files, not a replacement for clear design.

faq.question: Can it help refactor existing utility files?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current helper code and ask for a modular refactor.
